# Corporate Restructuring Concepts
## 1. Basic Definitions
- **Compromise**
    - Peaceful settlement of **Dispute**
    - Requires mutual adjustments
    - Precondition: Existence of a dispute
- **Arrangement**
    - Modification of Rights
    - **No Dispute** required
    - Scope: Wide (Includes Reorganization of Capital)
    - Parties: Members or Creditors

## 2. Reconstruction
- **Scope**
    - Usually involves only **One Company**
    - Reorganization of rights (Members/Creditors)
    - Business carried on in altered form
    - Methods: Reduction of capital, making partly paid shares, etc.

## 3. Amalgamation
- **Definition**
    - Blending of 2+ undertakings into one
    - Pursuant to Companies Act 2013
- **Parties**
    - **Transferor Co:** The company being amalgamated (Merged out)
    - **Transferee Co:** The company into which transferor is amalgamated (Merged in)

## 4. Types of Amalgamation
- **Type A: Nature of Merger**
    - **✅ Conditions (Must satisfy ALL 5)**
        - 1. **Assets/Liabilities:** **All** transferred to Transferee
        - 2. **Shareholders:** 📉 **≥90%** of Face Value of Equity Shares become shareholders of Transferee
        - 3. **Consideration:** Discharged **Wholly by Equity Shares** in Transferee
            - ⚠️ Exception: Cash allowed only for fractional shares
        - 4. **Business:** Intended to be **Carried On** by Transferee
        - 5. **Book Values:** **No Adjustment** to assets/liabilities values
            - ⚠️ Exception: Adjustments allowed only for uniformity of accounting policies
    - **📝 Accounting Method**
        - **Pooling of Interests Method**
        - Genuine pooling of assets/liabilities
- **Type B: Nature of Purchase**
    - **✅ Scope**
        - If **Any One** or more conditions of 'Merger' are **NOT** satisfied
    - **📝 Accounting Method**
        - **Purchase Method**
        - Only specific assets/liabilities taken over
        - Business usually not continued
# Chapter XV: Compromises, Arrangements & Amalgamations
## Sec 230: Power to Compromise/Arrangement
- **✅ Compliance**
    - **Tribunal Application**
        - By Co, Creditor, Member, or Liquidator
        - 🗳️ Tribunal orders meeting
    - **Disclosures (Affidavit) required while applying to the tribunal**
        - Material facts (Financials, Auditor Report, Pendency of investigation)
        - Reduction of Share Capital
        - Corporate Debt Restructuring Scheme (Consented by ≥75% Secured Creditors)
    - **Notice of Meeting**
        - Send to: Creditors, Members, Debenture-holders
        - Send to: Sectoral Regulators (RBI, SEBI, CCI, IT, ROC, OL)
        - ⏳ Reps by Regulators: Within 30 days (Else presumed nil)
        - Advertisement: Website + Newspaper
    - Annexure of the Notice to contain
        - Details of the arrangement/compromise
        - valuation report
        - Effect of the arrangement to the stakeholders
    - **Approval (The "Dual" Test)**
        - 🗳️ More than 50% of the people present and voting must agree
        - The people voting "Yes" must hold at least 75% of the total value of debt or shares represented at the meeting.
        - Voting: Person / Proxy / Postal Ballot
    - **Sanctioning Order**
        - To contain the follows
            - Conversion of Preference shares
                 - Option to be given for arrears of dividend in equity or cash
            - Protection of creditor rights
            - Pending proceedings before BIFD
            - Other matters like exit offer for dissenting shareholders
        - 📝 Auditor Certificate filed (Accounting Standards compliance u/s 133)
        - 📝 File Order with ROC within ⏳ 30 days
- **📉 Scope**
    - **Objection Threshold**
        - Persons holding 📉 ≥10% Shareholding OR
        - Persons with 📉 ≥5% Outstanding Debt
    - **Creditor Meeting Dispensation**
        - If 📉 ≥90% Creditors (in value) agree by affidavit
    - **Inclusions**
        - Buy-back (Sec 68 compliance mandatory)
        - Takeover Offer (SEBI regs for listed)

## Sec 231: Power of Tribunal to Enforce
- ** ✅ Compliance
    - Tribunal supervises implementation
    - Power to give directions / modifications into the scheme of arrangement
-  🚔 Liability
    - If scheme unworkable & Co unable to pay debts
    - ➡️ Tribunal orders Winding Up (Sec 273)

## Sec 232: Merger & Amalgamation

### (1) Application to Tribunal
- For Merger / Amalgamation / Division
- 🗳️ Tribunal orders Meeting of Creditors

### (2) Info Circulation
- Draft Scheme
- ROC Filing Confirmation
- Directors' Report (Share Exchange Ratio)
- Expert Valuation Report
- Supp. Accounting info (if first meeting for approval of scheme is > 6 months old)

### (3) Tribunal Order - to contain the follows
- Transfer of Assets/Liabilities
- Share Allotment (No Treasury Stock)
- Continuation of Legal Proceedings
- Dissolution without Winding Up
- Dissenting Shareholders' Provision
- Employee Transfer
- Set-off of Fees (Auth Capital)
- **Listed to Unlisted:** Exit option mandatory
- **Condition:** 📝 Auditor Certificate (AS compliance) required

### (4) Transfer Effect
- Assets/Liabilities legally transferred
- Property freed from charges (if ordered)

### (5) Filing
- 📝 File Order with ROC within ⏳ 30 days of receipt of the certified copy

### (6) Effective Date to be mentioned in the scheme

### (7) Annual Statement of compliance with the scheme 
- File yearly until completion of scheme
- Certified by CA/CS/CMA

### (8) Penalty
- 💰 20k + 1k/day (Max 3L)

## Sec 233: Fast Track Merger
- **📉 Scope**
    - 2 or more Small Companies
    - Holding Co & Wholly Owned Subsidiary (WOS)
    - Start-up Companies
- **✅ Compliance/ Pre Requisite**
    - **Notice**
        - To ROC & OL (Invite objections within ⏳ 30 days)
    - **Approval**
        - Members: 📉 ≥90% of total shares
        - Creditors: 📉 9/10th in value (Meeting 21 days notice)
    - **Solvency**
        - 📝 File Declaration of Solvency with ROC
    - **Filing Scheme**
        - Transferee files with CG (RD), ROC, OL
- Approval Mechanism (Sub-sec 3 & 4)
    - **No Objections**
        - RD registers scheme
        - Issues Confirmation Order
    - **Objections Exist**
        - RoC/OL must write to RD within 30 days
        - **Silence = Deemed Approval** (if no reply in 30 days)
    - RD Actions (Sub-sec 5)
        - **Scenario A: Public Interest/Creditors Protected**
            - RD confirms scheme
        - **Scenario B: Against Public Interest**
            - RD files application to NCLT (within 60 days)
            - Requests transfer to Normal Route (Sec 232)
    - NCLT Role (Sub-sec 6)
        - **Option 1:** Reject Fast Track -> Direct to Sec 232
        - **Option 2:** Dismiss RD's concern -> Confirm Scheme
- Registrar Confirmation
    - The order is to be filed with ROC
- Registration Effect
    - **Dissolution:** Transferor dissolved without winding up
    - **Assets/Liabilities:** Transferred to Transferee
    - **Legal Cases:** Continued against Transferee
    - **Treasury Shares:** Cancelled (Cannot hold own shares)
    - **Fees:** Set-off allowed (Transferor's fee deducted)
- Flexibility (Sub-sec 14)
    - Companies **can opt out** of Sec 233
    - Can choose Normal Route (Sec 230-232) at their discretion

## Sec 234: Cross Border Merger
- **📉 Scope**
    - Indian Co + Foreign Co (Notified Jurisdictions)
- **✅ Compliance**
    - Prior Approval of RBI
    - Comply with Sec 230-232
    - Consideration: Cash OR Depository Receipts
- Cross-border demergers are not permitted

## Sec 235: Dissenting Shareholders (Takeover)
- **📉 Scope**
    - Scheme approved by 📉 9/10th (90%) value of shares
    - Approval to be given within ⏳ 4 months of offer
- **✅ Compliance**
    - **Acquisition Notice**
        - Transferee notice to Dissenters within ⏳ 2 months (Form CAA-14)
    - **Dissenters' Right**
        - Apply to Tribunal within ⏳ 1 month of receipt of the notice - stating why such acquisition should not happen
    - **Outcome**
        - If after 1 month notice and after dropping dissenter's against proceedings, 
            - Transferee bound to acquire
            - Transferor registers transferee
            - Consideration held in Separate Bank A/c (Trust)
            - Disburse within ⏳ 60 days
    - Replace shares as applicable 

## Sec 236: Purchase of Minority Shareholding
- **📉 Scope**
     - Acquirer/PAC(Person acting in concert) holds 📉 ≥90% Equity
- **✅ Compliance**
    - **Notification**
        - Notify Co of intention to buy remaining
    - **Offer**
        - To minority at price by Registered Valuer
    - **Deposit**
        - Majority deposits amount in Separate Bank A/c
        - Operate that account for at least 1 year (That account should be open for 1 year)
        - Disburse within ⏳ 60 days
        - Company is the transfer agent
    - **Non Delivery of share certificate-**
        - If minority shareholder does not give share certificate, it is ok
        - it is deemed they are cancelled and the payment is to be made and shares are deemed to be transferred (Pay out of the deposit made by the majority guy)
    - **Additional Comp**
        - If 75% or more of the minority negotiates higher price later -> Share it pro rata with other minority
    - **Residual Minority**
        - Right to sell continues for ⏳ 3 years from the date of acquisition
        - The Majority cannot just wait for the time limit to run out or delist the company to trap the minority shareholders. The minority retains the right to demand that their shares be bought out.
## Sec 237: Amalgamation in Public Interest
- **📉 Scope**
    - Satisfaction of Central Govt (CG)
- **✅ Compliance**
    - CG issues Order in Official Gazette
    - Copy to companies (Modification suggestions invited)
    - Interest/Rights of members/creditors is protected
        - Any person who is aggreived?
            - Appeal to NCLT within 30 days of gazette order
    - Assessment of Compensation (Appeal to Tribunal within ⏳ 30 days)
    - Order laid before Parliament
- **Order Can be passed only when**
    - Proposed order is sent in draft to both companies
    - Time for appeal has expired
    - 2 months wait to receive suggestions from relevant stakeholders
    - Order to be laid out in both parliament houses

## Section 238: Registration of Offer of Schemes
### 1. Mandatory Registration (Sub-sec 1)
- **What to Register:** Copy of Circular or Offer regarding share transfer
- **Where:** With Registrar of Companies (RoC)
- **When:** **Before** issuing the offer to shareholders
### 2. Power to Refuse (Sub-sec 2)
- **Who:** Registrar (RoC)
- **Grounds for Refusal:**
  - Does not contain required information
  - Gives a false or misleading impression
- **Procedure:** RoC must communicate refusal + reasons
- **Time Limit:** Within **30 days** of receiving the document
### 3. Appeal Mechanism (Sub-sec 3)
- **Who:** Aggrieved Party (The Company)
- **Against:** Refusal Order by RoC
- **Authority:** Tribunal (NCLT)
- **Time Limit:** Within **30 days** of the refusal order
## Section 239: Preservation of Books & Papers
### Restriction of disposal
- Books/Papers of amalgamated or acquired company **shall not be disposed of**
- Requires **Prior Permission** of Central Government
### Before giving permission,
- Government appoints an inspector to check
    - affairs of company does not commit offence
    - Evidence is not destroyed in guise of merger
## Sec 240: Liability of Officers
- **🚔 Liability**
    - Offences prior to merger
    - Liability continues retrospectively (post-merger)
    - Merger is not a shield for past crimes
